channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: IAF’s Firm Backing Positions HAL’s HLFT-42 as Cornerstone of Post-2035 Training and Combat Doctrine
item.description: With Hindustan Aeronautics Limited receiving unequivocal backing from the Indian Air Force, the HLFT-42 programme is emerging as one of the most consequential indigenous aviation projects aimed at reshaping India’s pilot training and light combat ecosystem beyond 2035. According to information shared with idrw.org, despite the programme not yet clearing its Critical Design Review (CDR) milestone, the IAF has already indicated intent for firm orders of at least 100 aircraft, with the final number potentially going higher.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s New Submarine Coating Technology Improves Stealth and Survivability in Naval Warfare
item.description: India’s push toward self-reliance in critical naval technologies has seen steady progress, and one of the most strategically significant yet less-discussed developments is the indigenous production of rubber tiles for submarines. These specialized synthetic tiles, applied to the outer hull of submarines, play a decisive role in enhancing acoustic stealth—one of the most crucial factors in underwater warfare.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Advanced Assembly Jig at Nashik Set to Streamline Tejas Mk1A Fighter Production
item.description: India’s push to accelerate the production of its indigenous Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas Mk1A received a major boost last year after Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) operationalised a third production line equipped with advanced indigenous assembly infrastructure. The new facility, established at HAL’s Nashik division, significantly expands the production ecosystem for the Tejas programme and increases the overall manufacturing capacity to 24 aircraft per year, a key milestone for meeting the Indian Air Force’s growing requirements.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Media Leak Triggers Political Storm in Indonesia Over US Military Plan
item.description: A major geopolitical controversy has erupted after a classified U.S. proposal granting American military aircraft unrestricted access to Indonesian airspace was leaked just a day before a key defence agreement between Washington and Jakarta was due to be signed on April 13. The fallout forced both sides to exclude the overflight access clause from the final agreement, significantly altering the scope of what was intended to be a major strategic breakthrough in the Indo-Pacific.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Researcher Flags Inlet Design Constraints in Tejas Mk1 After Dubai Incident
item.description: A new technical study byAyush Sreejith, an independent researcher from Kerala, has brought fresh analytical insight into the loss of an Indian Air Force Tejas Mk1 during the Dubai Airshow in November 2025, pointing toward potential aerodynamic and thermal limitations in the aircraft’s inlet design under extreme flight conditions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: F-47 vs F-22: Why the US Air Force Is Betting on a ‘Swarm Leader’ Fighter
item.description: The Boeing F-47, the crewed fighter component of the U.S. Air Force’s Next Generation Air Dominance (NGAD) program, is rapidly advancing through its engineering phase following its formal designation and contract award in March 2025. Positioned as a sixth-generation platform, the F-47 is expected to redefine air combat through a combination of all-aspect stealth, adaptive propulsion, and AI-driven mission systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Why Taiwan Should Champion India’s Claim on Arunachal Pradesh to Strengthen Ties with the Indian People
item.description: In an era of shifting global alliances and rising tensions in the Indo-Pacific region, Taiwan finds itself at a crossroads. As China continues to assert its territorial ambitions, not just over Taiwan but across borders like those in Arunachal Pradesh, the island nation has a unique opportunity to align with India. By publicly supporting India’s sovereignty over Arunachal Pradesh—a region China claims as part of South Tibet—Taiwan could foster deeper diplomatic, economic, and cultural bonds. This move would not only counter Beijing’s expansionism but also resonate deeply with the Indian populace, turning geopolitical strategy into genuine goodwill.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Paras Defence ties up with Bandak Aviation for air-to-air refuelling system Sale and Support in India
item.description: On April 15, 2026, Paras Defence and Space Technologies Ltd. officially entered into a 10-year exclusive agreement with US-based Bandak Aviation Inc. (doing business as Northstar). This strategic partnership is focused on supplying and supporting advanced air-to-air refuelling systems and accessories for the Indian Armed Forces.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, Austria To Strengthen Partnership In Defence, Semiconductors And Quantum: PM Modi
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday announced that India and Austria will strengthen their partnership in defence, semiconductors, quantum, and biotechnology. Addressing a joint press meet with visiting Austrian Chancellor Christian Stocker, PM Modi stated that India and Austria will ensure reliable technology and supply chains for the entire world by combining Austria’s expertise with India’s speed and scale.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Military conflict cannot resolve problems: PM Modi on West Asia, Ukraine
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday called for the peaceful resolution of the conflicts in West Asia and Ukraine. He made the remarks after talks with Austria’s Federal Chancellor Christian Stocker here. “Today, the entire world is passing through a very serious and tense situation,” PM Modi said, adding, the impact of the tense situation in the world is being felt by all of us.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Faridabad To Nashik: Changing Profile Of Terror Modules Raises Security Concern
item.description: The Faridabad module case linked to the Delhi car bomb blast in November last year has served as a stark wake-up call for Indian security agencies. While several white-collar modules have been uncovered in the past, the Faridabad network operated on a far larger and more organised scale.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: South Korean President Lee to make state visits to India and Vietnam
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung will make state visits to India and Vietnam between April 19 and 24, a presidential adviser said on Thursday.Lee will start his trip in New Delhi at the invitation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, presidential adviser Wi Sung-lac told a briefing, according to the Blue House website.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India-Austria Partnership To Get More Innovation-Centric And Future-Ready: PM Modi
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday expressed confidence that the partnership between India and Austria will get “more innovation-centric and future-ready”. PM Modi’s statement came after he met Austrian Chancellor Christian Stocker and discussed ways to deepen cooperation in areas like innovation, infrastructure, and sustainability.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India-Zambia talks on critical minerals stall over mining rights concerns
item.description: India’s talks with Zambia over critical minerals mining have stalled amid a lack of assurances ?from Lusaka on mining rights, two sources familiar with ?the matter told Reuters. India last year received an allocation of 9,000 ?square km (3,474.92 square miles) to explore cobalt – a key component in batteries for electric vehicles and mobile phones – as well as copper, widely used in power generation, electronics and construction.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ISI-backed terror module busted, one held with 4 grenades: Punjab Police
item.description: The Punjab Police on Thursday claimed to have busted a terror module backed by Pakistan’s ISI with the arrest of one person and recovery of four hand grenades. In a joint operation of the State Special Operations cells of Amritsar and Mohali, the Counter Intelligence Wing of the Punjab Police also recovered two foreign-made pistols along with ammunition, Director General of Police Gaurav Yadav said.
item.dc:creator: admin
